summary: Smoking Help>Slip>Franklin’s jam, stellar Eyes Of The World
blurb: The show features a blazing Help > Slip > Franklin's sequence that rolls into a powerful Terrapin Station and a soaring Eyes Of The World. Jerry’s guitar shines throughout, with Vince delivering solid keyboard runs on the jams. Overall the performance is tight, jam‑heavy and one of the stronger shows of early ’91.
review: On February 21, 1991 at the Oakland‑Alameda County Coliseum the Dead delivered a high‑octane set that still resonates with fans. The first set kicked off with an incendiary Help > Slip > Franklin’s medley that many reviewers call "smoking" and "one of the best Slipknot jams" of the era, showcasing Jerry ripping his guitar and Vince laying down heavy organ runs. After a brief interlude of bluesy Memphis Blues and classic rockers like Tennessee Jed and The Promised Land, the second set unfolded into a seamless flow: Playing In The Band into Uncle John’s Band, then a soaring Terrapin Station that segued into the dual drumming extravaganza, Space, and an unforgettable Eyes Of The World where Jerry's solo was described as “playing his heart out.” The band stayed locked in through Throwing Stones and closed with a heartfelt Knockin’ On Heaven’s Door. With solid keyboard work, tight rhythm section, and that signature Dead improvisational spirit, this concert stands out as a highlight of the early ’90s run.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Two recordings (soundboard and audience) are clearly positive, praising the Help > Slip > Franklin’s jam, Eyes Of The World, Terrapin Station and overall high energy; a third recording is mixed but offers no criticism. Consistent acclaim across multiple sources warrants a strong positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Clear soundboard capture with crisp mix, praised for highlighting the key jams and minimal technical issues.

key_highlights: Help > Slip > Franklin’s sequence delivers massive improvisation.
Terrapin Station flows into Space and a soaring Eyes Of The World.
One of the era's best Slipknot! jams, highlighted by Vince’s keyboard runs.
Overall tight, jam‑heavy performance with standout guitar work.
song_highlights: Stuck Inside of Mobile with the Memphis Blues Again

band_performance.Jerry: Ripping on guitar throughout, especially during the Eyes Of The World solo and the Help > Slip > Franklin’s jam; described as playing his heart out.
band_performance.Vince: Heavy yet solid organ/keyboard work with great runs on Franklin’s, Memphis Blues and the Slipknot sequence.

must_listen_sequences: Stuck Inside of Mobile with the Memphis Blues Again
